title: Method for preparing an alloy of silver and copper for articles
abstract: A method (100) of preparing an alloy of a silver and a copper for articles is disclosed, comprising at step (102) checking purity of a silver raw material and a copper raw material, at step (104), heating the silver of a specified purity of 99.9% pure and the copper of a specified purity of 98.98% to a temperature of a red hot condition, at step (106), quenching the heated silver and the heated copper in a sesame oil, a buttermilk, a sour gruel, a cow urine, and a decoction of dolichos biflorus, at step (108), melting a mixture of the quenched silver and the quenched copper, and at step (110), cooling the molten mixture of the silver, and the copper to form the alloy, wherein at the step (106). At the step (106), the silver gets purified about 99.52% to 99.99% and the copper gets purified about 99.85% to 99.9%..
claims: Claims I Claim: 1. A method for preparing an alloy of a silver and a copper, the method comprising steps of: checking purity of a silver raw material and a copper raw material by any of an electron microscopy and an energy dispersive X-ray spectroscopy separately; heating the silver of a specified purity of about 99.9% pure and the copper of a specified purity of about 98.98% pure to a temperature of a red hot condition separately; quenching, one or more times, the heated silver and the heated copper in liquid mediums such as a sesame oil, a buttermilk, a sour gruel, a cow urine, and a decoction of dolichos biflorus; melting a mixture of the quenched silver and the quenched copper in a specific proportion; and cooling the molten mixture of the silver and the copper to form the alloy of the silver and the copper, wherein at the step of quenching the silver and the cooper in the liquid mediums the silver gets purified in a range of about 99.95% to 99.99% and the copper gets purified in a range of about 99.85% to 99.9%. 2. The method as claimed in claim 1, wherein the alloy comprises the silver in a range of about 80% to 90%, and the copper in a range of about 10% to 20%. 3. The method as claimed in claim 1, wherein at the step of heating the silver and the copper are heated to the temperature in a range of about 450 degree Celsius (Â°C) to 700 Â°C. 4. The method as claimed in claim 1, wherein the step of quenching of the silver and the copper leads to decrease in pH value of the silver and the copper, and increase in corrosive resistance property of the silver and the copper, and wherein the step of quenching of the silver and the copper also leads to removal of acidic soluble impurities and basic soluble impurities from the silver and the copper. 5. The method as claimed in claim 1, wherein the step of heating of the silver and the copper and the step of melting of the silver and the copper are performed by using a furnace heating system. 6. The claimed as claimed in claim 1, wherein the step of quenching of the heated silver and the heated copper are performed separately in the separate liquid medium. 7. The method as claimed in claim 1, wherein the silver raw material and copper raw material are in any form such as an ingot, a rod, and a wire. 8. An alloy for an article, said alloy comprising: a silver in a range of about 80 % to 90 %; and a copper in a range of about 10 % to 20 %, wherein the silver of a specified purity of about 99.9% pure and the copper of a specified purity of about 98.98% pure are independently purified by quenching in liquid mediums such as a sesame oil, a buttermilk, a sour gruel, a cow urine, and a decoction of dolichos bifloms to form the alloy of the silver having purity in a range of about 99.95% to 99.99% pure and the copper having purity in a range of about 99.85% to 99.9% pure. 9. The alloy as claimed in claim 8, wherein the alloy of the purified silver and the purified copper is corrosive resistance. 10. The alloy as claimed in claim 8, wherein the alloy of the purified silver and the purified copper includes very less acidic soluble impurities and basic soluble impurities.
